top_risks: CLIENT-REJECT (sev 9): The data files are delivered as JSON objects, not CSVs — the InDesign Data Merge panel and the document_merge_data_layout connector both require flat .csv files with exact column-header rows. No CSV was actually handed over, so every data-merge step is unbindable before work even begins.

AGENT CHOKE on 'team_roster_csv': document_merge_data_layout expects a linked .csv with a header row matching the exact field names (full_name, title, email, phone, pronouns). The asset is a JSON object; the agent has no CSV to point the merge panel at, so it will either throw a file-type error or silently produce cards with every merge field unpopulated — blocking all six business-card records from rendering.
